articles.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers 'galaxy-killing' wind that may explain why some early galaxies lived fast and died young
articles.summary: A “galaxy-killing” wind driven by cosmic mergers may explain why many massive galaxies in the early universe stopped forming stars far earlier than expected, according to new JWST and ALMA observations.
